questions.question:
Let the characteristic equation of matrix \( M \) be \( \lambda ^{2} - \lambda - 1 = 0 \). Then.



TIFR CSE 2010 | Part A | Question: 16
options: \( M^{-1} \) exists but cannot be determined from the data.
\( M^{-1} \) exists and can be determined from the data but the choices (c) and (d) are incorrect.

questions.question:
Let \( A \) be a matrix such that \( A^{k}=0 \). What is the inverse of \( I - A \)?



TIFR CSE 2012 | Part B | Question: 12

How many \( 4 \times 4 \) matrices with entries from \( {0, 1} \) have odd determinant?

Hint: Use modulo \( 2 \) arithmetic.



TIFR CSE 2013 | Part B | Question: 3

Consider the following \( 3 \times 3 \) matrices.

M_{1}=\begin{pmatrix}   
0&1&1 \\
1&0&1  \\
1&1&0 
\end{pmatrix}

M_{2}=\begin{pmatrix}   
1&0&1 \\
0&0&0  \\
1&0&1 
\end{pmatrix}

How may \( 0-1 \) column vectors of the form

\( X \)= \begin{pmatrix}
x_{1} \\
x_{2} \\
x_{3}
\end{pmatrix}      

are there such that \( M_{1}X = M_{2}X \) (modulo \( 2 \))? (modulo \( 2 \) means all operations are done modulo \( 2 \), i.e, \( 3 = 1 \) (modulo \( 2 \)), \( 4 = 0 \) (modulo \( 2 \))).



TIFR CSE 2015 | Part A | Question: 14

Let \( A \) be am \( n\times n \) invertible matrix with real entries whose column sums are all equal to \( 1 \). Consider the following statements:

  1. Every column in the matrix \( A^{2} \) sums to \( 2 \)
  2. Every column in the matrix \( A^{3} \) sums to \( 3 \)
  3. Every column in the matrix \( A^{-1} \) sums to \( 1 \)

Which of the following is TRUE?



TIFR CSE 2020 | Part A | Question: 5
options: statement \( (1) \) is correct but not statements \( (2) \) or \( (3) \)
statement \( (2) \) is correct but not statements \( (1) \) or \( (3) \)
statement \( (3) \) is correct but not statements \( (1) \) or \( (2) \)
questions.correct_answer: statement \( (3) \) is correct but not statements \( (1) \) or \( (2) \)

questions.question:
Consider the \( n \times n \) matrix \( M \) defined as follows:
\[ M=\left(\begin{array}{cccc}
1 & 2 & \ldots & n \\
n+1 & n+2 & \ldots & 2 n \\
2 n+1 & 2 n+2 & \ldots & 3 n \\
\vdots & \vdots & \ldots & \vdots \\
(n-1) n+1 & (n-1) n+2 & \ldots & n^{2}
\end{array}\right). \]
Let \( M_{i, j} \) denote the entry present in the \( i \)-th row and the \( j \)-th column of \( M \) for each \( 1 \leq i, j \leq n \). Define a set \( S \) as
\[
S=\left\{M_{i_{1}, j_{1}}+M_{i_{2}, j_{2}}+\cdots+M_{i_{n}, j_{n}} \mid\left(i_{1}, \ldots, i_{n}\right) \text { and }\left(j_{1}, \ldots, j_{n}\right)\right.
\]
are permutations of \( [n]\} \).

(Here, \( [n] \) denotes the set \( \{1,2, \ldots, n\} \) of positive integers from \( 1 \) to \( n.) \) How many elements does \( S \) have?



TIFR CSE 2023 | Part A | Question: 15

Suppose \( A \) is a \( 2 \times 2 \) matrix such that the sum of the principal diagonal entries of \( A \) is \( 10 \) and the sum of the principal diagonal entries of \( A^{2} \) is \( 20 \). (For any \( 2 \times 2 \) matrix \( B \), the principal diagonal entries of \( B \) are the entries \( B_{1,1} \) and \( B_{2,2}.) \) What is \( \operatorname{det}(A)? \) 



TIFR CSE 2023 | Part A | Question: 5
options: Nonzero, but cannot be uniquely determined from the above data.
Cannot be uniquely determined from the above data, and could also be zero.

questions.question:
Let \( \text{A} \) and \( \text{B} \) be two \( n \times n \) invertible matrices with real entries such that every row in \( \text{A} \) sums to \( 1 \) and every row in \( \text{B} \) sums to \( 2 \). Consider the following three statements:

  1. Every row in the matrix \( \text{AB} \) sums to \( 2 \).
  2. Every row in the matrix \( \text{A}^{-1} B \) sums to \( 2 \).
  3. Every row in the matrix \( \text{A}^{-1} \text{B}^{-1} \) sums to \( \frac{1}{2} \).

Which of the above statements is/are true?



TIFR CSE 2024 | Part A | Question: 14
options: All the three statements \( \text{(i), (ii)} \), and \( \text{(iii)} \) are true.
Statement \( \text{(i)} \) is true but not necessarily statements \( \text{(ii)} \) or \( \text{(iii)} \).
Statements \( \text{(i)} \) and \( \text{(ii)} \) are true but not necessarily statement \( \text{(iii)} \).
Statements \( \text{(i)} \) and \( \text{(iii)} \) are true but not necessarily statement \( \text{(ii)} \).
questions.correct_answer: All the three statements \( \text{(i), (ii)} \), and \( \text{(iii)} \) are true.
